perturbation.original_text: 2. TERM 2.1 The term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence on the date hereof (the "Effective Date") and shall expire upon delivery of [**] to ebix, but in no way shall this Agreement extend any later than thirty (30) months from the Effective Date regardless of the number Filled Application Forms delivered to ebix. About will make commercially reasonable efforts to achieve that number in twelve (12) months or less from the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. TERM 2.1 The term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence on the date hereof (the "Effective Date") and shall expire thirty (30) months from the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the clause related to delivery of [**] to ebix as a condition for the contract's expiry, but keeping the clause 'About will make commercially reasonable efforts to achieve that number in twelve (12) months or less from the Effective Date.', this creates an in-text contradiction. The contract now states that the term expires after 30 months, but a clause still exists implying an earlier termination based on a separate, now unmentioned, condition. This introduces uncertainty about when the Term ends, as Section 4.3 states, 'If this Agreement is terminated by either party pursuant to Sections 13.1 [TERMINATION AND SURVIVAL], 13.2 [TERMINATION AND SURVIVAL] or 13.3 [TERMINATION AND SURVIVAL] prior to the expiration of the Term of this Agreement, About shall be obligated to deliver only a pro-rated number of impressions and Filled application forms to ebix'. This implies that the Term could end before the 30 months but it is not clearly stated in section 2.1, generating an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3 If this Agreement is terminated by either party pursuant to Sections 13.1 [TERMINATION AND SURVIVAL], 13.2 [TERMINATION AND SURVIVAL] or 13.3 [TERMINATION AND SURVIVAL] prior to the expiration of the Term of this Agreement, About shall be obligated to deliver only a pro-rated number of impressions and Filled application forms to ebix
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits a condition for the contract's expiry (delivery of [**] to ebix), but retains language suggesting an earlier termination is possible ('About will make commercially reasonable efforts to achieve that number in twelve (12) months or less from the Effective Date.'). This directly contradicts section 4.3, which refers to termination 'prior to the expiration of the Term of this Agreement.' The omission creates uncertainty as to the term's length. The original text clearly specified an earlier termination trigger. By deleting that trigger but keeping the reference to potentially early termination elsewhere (Section 4.3), creates an in-text contradiction as to the terms of termination and length of the contract. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2 In addition, ebix agrees to pay to About a fee of [**] for [**] and [**] from [**] up to [**] (the "Application Fees"). The payment of the Application Fees are to be due within 30 days after the end of the month in which the Application Fees were generated. Such payments shall be accompanied by a report stating the number of Filled Application Forms generated during the previous month, as well as the amount due to About. Ebix will track the number of Filled Application Forms generated by About Customers.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 In addition, ebix agrees to pay to About a fee (the "Application Fees"). The payment of the Application Fees are to be due within 30 days after the end of the month in which the Application Fees were generated. Such payments shall be accompanied by a report stating the number of Filled Application Forms generated during the previous month, as well as the amount due to About. Ebix will track the number of Filled Application Forms generated by About Customers.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the explicit amount of the Application Fees, the contract retains the requirement for payment but lacks a specific figure. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 5.3 then refers to 'If any of the payments are not received on or before the appropriate dates as described above'. Since the 'amount' to be paid has been omitted from Section 5.2, enforcing the penalties in Section 5.3 becomes problematic, leading to uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3 If any of the payments are not received on or before the appropriate dates as described above, About shall have the right to charge ebix interest on the overdue amount at the rate of 6% per year,, calculated from 30 days after the end of the month for which payment is made until the date of ebix's payment of such amount, which interest shall be in addition to such fees due and owing About. In addition, About shall be entitled to its reasonable costs and expenses (including attorneys' fees) in connection with any action to collect fees under or to otherwise enforce this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the explicit amount for the Application Fees in Section 5.2 creates a contradiction with Section 5.3. Section 5.3 refers to interest on the 'overdue amount,' but without a specified amount in Section 5.2, there is no determinable 'overdue amount.' This renders the interest calculation in Section 5.3 unenforceable due to the omission in 5.2, as the contract now lacks a specific monetary figure for the Application Fees.

perturbation.original_text: 13.1 eBix may terminate this Agreement, [**] upon [**] to About; provided, however, that the termination is no earlier than [**] of the Effective Date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.1 eBix may terminate this Agreement; provided, however, that the termination is no earlier than [**] of the Effective Date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the reason and conditions for termination of this Agreement by Ebix, the contract loses clarity on Ebix's right to terminate the agreement. By Omitting the reason and condition '[**] upon [**] to About;' for Ebix to terminate, whilst keeping the condition stating 'provided, however, that the termination is no earlier than [**] of the Effective Date of this Agreement.', generates the assumption that there may be other conditions that might be valid, or not. This creates an in-text contradiction, because in 13.6 there is still reffering to termination of this agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.6 Upon termination of this Agreement, any and all licenses granted by one party to the other hereunder shall immediately terminate, and the parties shall immediately cease the use of the other party's Marks, material or content provided in connection with this Agreement, and shall remove all Links to and from one another's Sites, and About shall remove all Links between the About Network and the Insurance Center. Each party shall return the Confidential Information of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified conditions under which eBix could terminate the agreement. The changed text removes those conditions, creating ambiguity about eBix's termination rights. Clause 13.6 refers to 'Upon termination of this Agreement', and by removing the explicit conditions for eBix's termination in 13.1, a contradiction is created. It becomes unclear under what specific circumstances the provisions of 13.6 are triggered regarding eBix, because section 13.1 became ambigious. Therefore, there is a direct conflict and inconsistency, as the removal of the explicit conditions in 13.1 impacts the clear application of 13.6.
